mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off selling that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in >70% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment spike, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous peaks in retail sales and housing starts indicate demand-side overheating that forces central banks to accelerate tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high percentiles of RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52% or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening corporate output and revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when inflation expectations remain anchored.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5%, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 290 signal entrenched inflation that compels aggressive monetary policy responses, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 50% or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 or directional accuracy at or below 50% across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side disruption and weakening economic activity, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in >65% of instances following a 1% monthly INDPRO decline, or the threshold signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.
